scenarios.opening_query: My partner drained our joint savings for a 'sure thing' investment that predictably vanished.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements || Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.)
scenarios.hidden_fact: We're married — twelve years — and this is the last straw; the dissipation claim belongs inside the divorce I'm ready to file.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We're married twelve years — and this is the last straw. The dissipation claim goes inside the divorce I'm filing.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We never married — the account was joint by convenience, and untangling contributions is the unmarried-partner fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Never married — the account was joint by convenience, and it's the contributions fight.

scenarios.opening_query: My partner was rushed into surgery and the hospital wouldn't tell me anything for six hours.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements || Wills & Trusts > Power of Attorney
scenarios.hidden_fact: We're unmarried by choice — what we need now is the document set: healthcare POAs, directives, and authorizations naming each other, so this never happens again.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We're unmarried by choice — we need the documents: healthcare POAs and authorizations naming each other.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether planning documents are the remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's worse — his estranged sister used the gap to move him to another facility and cut me off entirely; contesting her control is the fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: His estranged sister used the gap to move him and cut me off — contesting her control is the fight.

scenarios.opening_query: We ran a food cart together for five years and the breakup means someone gets the cart and the permits.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The cart runs through an LLC we both own 50/50 — the split is a member buyout with permit transfers, entity work more than relationship work.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The cart's in an LLC we own 50/50 — it's a member buyout with permit transfers.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a formal entity structures the split
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No entity — everything's in his name though I paid half; it's the classic unmarried-partner equity fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No entity — his name on everything, my money in half of it.

scenarios.opening_query: My late partner's family is contesting everything he left me even though his will is crystal clear.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements || Wills & Trusts > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: There IS a will naming me — the family filed a contest claiming undue influence, so it's a will-contest defense, with our relationship as evidence, not the theory.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: There's a will naming me — they filed a contest claiming undue influence. It's will-contest defense.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a valid will makes this a contest defense
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no will — we never got to it — so my claims rest on our fourteen years of joint contributions against intestacy.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No will — so my claims rest on fourteen years of joint contributions against intestacy.

scenarios.opening_query: My girlfriend's ex keeps claiming rights to the condo she and I live in and now there's a court letter.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements || Real Property > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: His name is still on the deed from when they bought it — the letter is his partition action, a co-ownership suit she has to answer regardless of our relationship.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's still on the deed from their purchase — the letter's his partition action, and she has to answer it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether deed co-ownership makes it a partition case
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He was never on title — his 'claim' is that he paid part of her down payment while they dated; a contribution dispute, not a title case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He was never on title — he just claims down-payment money from their dating years.

scenarios.opening_query: After eight years together my partner left, and yesterday I got served with an eviction from my own home.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements || Real Property > Tenant (Residential)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The house is solely hers and she filed a formal eviction case — whatever my equity claims, the eviction has its own deadline and defenses that come first.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The house is solely hers and it's a formal eviction case — that deadline and defense come first, equity claims after.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a filed eviction sets the immediate clock
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'eviction' is a letter from her lawyer, not a case — and the deed shows both our names, which changes everything.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a lawyer letter, not a filed case — and we're BOTH on the deed.
